steps.title: Quick question first…
steps.question: A step-up transformer…
options: Has more turns on the primary coil than the secondary coil
Increases the voltage and increases the current by the same factor
Has more turns on the secondary coil than the primary coil
steps.misconceptionFeedback: ⚠️ Common misconception! A step-up transformer has more turns on the secondary coil than the primary. It increases voltage but — for an ideal transformer — decreases current proportionally to conserve power. Transformers only work with AC, not DC.
steps.correctFeedback: ✓ Correct! A step-up transformer has more secondary turns (ns > np), which gives a higher output voltage. Current decreases to conserve power.
steps.bestSource: BEST resource: AQA Physics specification 4.7.5 — Transformers

steps.title: Transformers
steps.bodyHTML: A transformer uses electromagnetic induction to change the voltage of an alternating current. It consists of two coils (primary and secondary) wound around a soft iron core. The ratio of voltages equals the ratio of turns: Vp/Vs = np/ns. A step-up transformer has more secondary turns and increases voltage; a step-down transformer has fewer secondary turns and decreases voltage.
commonErrors: Thinking transformers work with DC — they require AC
Confusing which coil is primary and which is secondary
Forgetting that increasing voltage decreases current (power is conserved)

steps.title: See it in action
steps.instruction: Drag the sliders to change the number of primary turns and the primary voltage. Watch how the secondary voltage changes. Secondary turns are fixed at 200.

aqa.questionText: A transformer has 500 turns on the primary coil and 2000 turns on the secondary coil. The primary voltage is 230 V. Calculate the secondary (output) voltage.

steps.label: Identify the knowns
steps.content:
np = 500 turns
ns = 2000 turns
Vp = 230 V

steps.label: Rearrange and calculate
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — correct rearrangement; 1 mark — correct answer with unit

aqa.questionText: A transformer has a primary voltage of 400 V and 1000 turns on the primary coil. The secondary coil has 250 turns. What is the secondary voltage?
aqa.markSchemeAward: 1 mark equation · 1 mark substitution · 1 mark correct answer + unit
aqa.commonMistake: Option A results from dividing np by ns instead of ns by np — always multiply Vp by the ratio (ns/np).

equation.examTip: Step-up: ns > np so Vs > Vp. Step-down: ns < np so Vs < Vp. Transformers only work with AC.

boardNotes.aqa: On AQA formula sheet. Know how to rearrange for any of the four quantities.
